tluaMetatableCall.tlua(12,36): error TLUA2554: Expected 1 arguments, but got 2.
tluaMetatableCall.tlua(13,27): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.


==== tluaMetatableCall.tlua (2 errors) ====
    // __call makes a paired table callable. Lua passes the table itself as the metamethod's first
    // argument, so a call site supplies the arguments from the second parameter on.
    
    local callable = setmetatable({ n = 1 }, {
      __call = function(self: object, x: number): string return "s"; end,
    });
    
    local r: string = callable(2);
    local n: number = callable.n;       // the table's own members still read
    
    // The receiver is implicit, so passing it back is one argument too many.
    local tooMany = callable(callable, 2);
                                       ~
!!! error TLUA2554: Expected 1 arguments, but got 2.
    local wrongArg = callable("x");
                              ~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
    
    // A callable pairing is still a table: pairs iterates it.
    for k, v in pairs(callable) do
      local key: string = k;
    end
    
    // A __call whose only parameter is a rest keeps it -- the rest absorbs the receiver and the
    // arguments alike.
    local rest = setmetatable({}, { __call = function(...: any): number return 1; end });
    local rr: number = rest(1, "x");
    
    // A __call-only metatable is enough to pair, so getmetatable answers for it.
    local mt = getmetatable(callable);
